PLAINTIFF'S PETITION "FOR AN ORDER RELIEVING PLAINTIFF FROM THE CLAIM FILING REQUIREMENTS OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 945.4" IS DENIED AS MOOT (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2024 Continued To Sep-18-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition For A Hearing For Order Relieving Plaintiff From Provisions Of Government Code 945.4
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. PAUL COOK, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm, PLC (tel: 213-381-9988) for Petitioner.
Atty. CORY WILSON, Esq. of Bertrand, Fox, Elliot, Osman & Wenzel (tel: 415-353-0999) for Respondent.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER MARCUS ANGELES' Petition For A Hearing For Order Relieving Plaintiff From Provisions Of Government Code 945.4.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's petition "for an order relieving Plaintiff from the claim filing requirements of Government Code section 945.4" is denied as moot. (Petition, 1:23-24.) Section 945.4 provides that a government claim must be filed to perfect a subsequent court action for damages. Government Code section 946.6 allows the court to relieve the plaintiff from the failure to file a proper claim.

These provisions are inapposite because the record shows that plaintiff (through prior counsel Bora Song) in fact filed a claim on April 27, 2023, that referred to an attack on September 2, 2022, and the tennis racket incident of January 9, 2023. (Parkin Decl., Ex. A.) Defendant served a rejection of those claims on former counsel on June 9, 2023. (Parkin Decl., Ex. B.) Defendant explained that any claim for events prior to October 27, 2022, were untimely and rejected the claim for any conduct on or after October 27, 2022, (which includes the January 9, 2023, tennis racket attack). Plaintiff complains that the letter did not warn to seek the advice of counsel (see Government Code section 911.3), but defendant sent that letter to plaintiff's counsel of record. Based on plaintiff's petition, it appears that plaintiff only seeks to pursue the latter tennis racket incident.

Plaintiff further generally argues that defendant should be estopped from asserting noncompliance with the government claim act. Plaintiff notes that prior counsel represented that there were no documents in the file and defendant never informed present counsel that a claim was presented and rejected. This petition is not the proper vehicle to resolve these matters. Petitioner can file a complaint and subsequent proceedings will resolve whether the limitations period of Government Code section 945.6(a)(1) [file lawsuit within six months of rejection] bars the claim or whether estoppel applies.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Noelle Krawiec, CSR#14255 (tel: 661-607-5707/ e-mail: noelleckrawiec@gmail.com)
